meta.h1: Title 11 — Public Transit Maui Bus · Plain-Language Service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 11 (Public Transit) — the Maui Bus system: fixed routes, fares, and ADA paratransit. Run by the County Department of Transportation (operated under contract). Fares and routes change — figures marked expanding are being verified against the County's current schedule; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: 1 · The Maui Bus
sections.note: How public transit works on Maui under Title 11.
sections.html: The Maui Bus is the County's fixed-route public transit system — 12 routes operated 7 days a week (including holidays) across Central, South, West, Upcountry, Kula, and Haʻikū communities, run for the County by a contracted operator. The County has introduced a fixed-route fare-free transit program; check the current fare status and schedules on the County's Bus Service page before you ride.

source.html: County of Maui Maui Bus Public Transit System + Bus Service & Schedules; Dept. of Transportation (808) 270-7511.
source.expanding: current fare-free status + live route map

sections.title: 2 · Service & fares
sections.note: Historical fares shown for reference — a fare-free fixed-route program has been introduced, so confirm the current fare on the County page before relying on these.

source.html: County of Maui Bus Service Information + ADA Paratransit Program. Fares as previously published; the fixed-route fare-free program may supersede.

sections.title: 3 · ADA paratransit
sections.note: Door-to-door service for riders who can't use the fixed route.
sections.html: The County offers ADA paratransit — an advance-reservation, curb-to-curb service for persons with disabilities who are unable to use the regular fixed-route bus. Eligibility and reservations are handled by the Department of Transportation; the historical fare is $2.00 one-way (ticket books $20 for 12 trips).

source.html: County of Maui Maui Bus ADA Paratransit Service Program.

sections.title: 4 · Who pays — the charter lens
sections.note: Labeled analysis — public transit read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter, the Department of Transportation operates public transit as a County service, funded by fares plus County and federal subsidy; service levels and fares are policy choices of the administration and Council.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, mobility is a precondition for participating in community life — work, school, ʻohana, civic duty. The lens reads the move toward fare-free transit as restoring access as a shared right, especially for elders, youth, and low-income residents who depend on the bus.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Transit is access, and access is dignity. Fare-free, frequent service pays the community back many times over — the fixed-route fare-free program is the right direction. Same service, read two ways.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this page and the transit guide are Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a paratransit-eligibility helper and an AI transit assistant will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only).
